title: Fix Wants=/Requires= in quadlet example
body:
This used to say Wants=/Requires=startup-task.container, but these lines are copied into the produced unit file unmodified and systemd does not know about .container objects and throws something like:

    /run/systemd/generator/app.service:8: Failed to add dependency on startup-task.container, ignoring: Invalid argument

Instead, these directives should use the resulting .service name directly.

comments.body:
Can you please elaborate on the issue you've encountered that prompt this PR?
You are correct that systemd does not know about Quadlet units. However, [this](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/55542db3c547c288601ddb16b4398aaffa528759/pkg/systemd/quadlet/quadlet.go#L2294) translates the Quadlet units into their corresponding service units and returns an error when it fails to do so.
So, AFAIK the man page is OK, but there might be an issue.

I wrote a big comment explaining things, but I just realized the problem: I'm running podman 5.4 which does not have this translation feature yet, which explains why it was broken.

Also, the docs say:

> The Podman files use the same format as [regular systemd unit files](https://www.freedesktop.org/software/systemd/man/systemd.syntax.html). Each file type has a custom section (for example, [Container]) that is handled by Podman, and all other sections are passed on untouched, allowing the use of any normal systemd configuration options like dependencies or cgroup limits.

So I assumed "untouched" actually meant "untouched", but this is one exception. I'll see if I can submit a PR to change this wording slightly tomorrow (do you happen to know if there are more exceptions? I looked through the code, but it is too much too quickly tell).
